tools.name: Get News
tools.description: Get current news headlines. Categories: "general" (top headlines), "finance" (market news, economy), "tech" (technology, AI, startups), or "stock" (news about a specific stock ticker). For stock news, include the ticker symbol.

tools.name: Get Weather
tools.description: Get current weather for a city or location. Returns temperature, humidity, wind, and conditions. Use when user asks about the weather anywhere.

tools.name: Get Weather Forecast
tools.description: Get weather forecast for upcoming days. Use when user asks about weekend weather or future conditions.

tools.name: Get Sports
tools.description: Get sports scores, standings, and schedules. Query by team name (e.g., "Eagles", "Phillies", "Lakers") or league (NFL, MLB, NBA, NHL). Returns most recent game results, next scheduled game, and current standings.

tools.name: Search Web
tools.description: Search the internet for current information, facts, how-tos, or any topic. Returns relevant web results with summaries. Great for: "What is...", "How to...", "Latest news about...", or researching any topic.

tools.name: Lookup Info
tools.description: Quick lookup for definitions, facts, or encyclopedia-style information. Sources: Wikipedia for detailed topics, dictionary for word definitions. Best for: "Define...", "What does X mean?", "Who was...", factual questions.

tools.name: Bank Account
tools.description: Manage bank account connection: check link status, link a new bank account via Plaid, or unlink an existing account. Actions: "status", "link", or "unlink". Required for spending analysis and balance checking.

tools.name: Bank Data
tools.description: Get financial data from linked accounts: account balances, recent transactions, or spending analysis by category. Actions: "balances", "transactions", or "spending". Requires linked bank account.

tools.name: Financial Health
tools.description: Get a comprehensive financial health assessment based on linked account data. Shows savings rate, spending patterns, debt-to-income, and personalized recommendations.

tools.name: Investment Calculator
tools.description: Calculate investment growth, fee impact, or retirement projections. Modes: "growth" (compound interest over time), "fees" (how fees reduce returns), or "retirement" (project retirement savings). Uses Rule of 72 for quick estimates.

tools.name: Housing Calculator
tools.description: Calculate mortgage payments, total interest, or home affordability. Modes: "mortgage" (monthly payment and total cost), "affordability" (how much house you can afford based on income). Includes down payment scenarios.

tools.name: Savings Calculator
tools.description: Calculate emergency fund target, savings rate, or time to reach goals. Modes: "emergency" (3-6 month fund target), "rate" (what % of income you're saving), or "double" (years to double money at given rate).

tools.name: Debt Payoff Planner
tools.description: Calculate debt payoff timeline, total interest, and strategies. Supports: snowball method (smallest first), avalanche method (highest interest first), or custom. Shows monthly payment needed and payoff date.

tools.name: Budget Planner
tools.description: Create a budget using the 50/30/20 rule (needs/wants/savings), or explore what financial security might look like for you. Modes: "budget" (allocate income) or "security" (explore your personal financial freedom target).

tools.name: Finance Education
tools.description: Explain financial concepts in plain language. Topics: banking (APY, overdraft, etc.), mortgage (PMI, points, ARM vs fixed), retirement accounts (401k vs IRA, Roth vs Traditional), or investing (diversification, index funds, etc.).

tools.name: Start Game
tools.description:
Start a fun music game with the user!

Available games:
- "name-that-tune" - Play a song clip, user guesses the song/artist
- "one-word-song" - User says a word, you find a song with that word
- "desert-island-discs" - User picks 5 songs they'd bring to a desert island
- "this-or-that" - Play two songs, user picks their favorite
- "mood-dj-challenge" - User describes a mood, you pick the perfect song

Use when user says things like:
- "Let's play a game"
- "Play name that tune"
- "Let's do desert island discs"
- "Can we play a music game?"

tools.name: Submit Game Answer
tools.description:
Submit the user's answer or choice in the current game.

Use when:
- User guesses a song in Name That Tune
- User says a word in One Word Song
- User picks a song in Desert Island Discs
- User chooses A or B in This or That
- User describes a mood in Mood DJ Challenge
- User rates your song pick (1-5)

tools.name: Get Game Hint
tools.description:
Give the user a hint in the current game.
Use when user says "hint", "help", "I don't know", or seems stuck.

tools.name: Skip Game Round
tools.description:
Skip the current round in the game.
Use when user says "skip", "pass", "next", or wants to move on.

tools.name: End Game
tools.description:
End the current game early.
Use when user says "stop", "quit", "end game", or wants to do something else.

tools.name: Get Game Status
tools.description:
Check the current game status.
Use when user asks "what's the score?", "what round?", "how am I doing?"

tools.name: Get Game History
tools.description:
Check the user's game history and stats.
Use when user asks "how many games have I played?", "what's my best score?"

tools.name: Suggest Game
tools.description:
Suggest a game to the user based on context or mood.
Use proactively during lulls or when user seems like they want to do something fun.

tools.name: Start Text Game
tools.description:
Start a text-based game like tic-tac-toe with the user!

Available text games:
- "tic-tac-toe" - Classic 3x3 grid game. User says positions like "center", "top left", or numbers 1-9.

Use when user says things like:
- "Let's play tic tac toe"
- "Play tic-tac-toe with me"
- "Can we play a game?" (then offer tic-tac-toe as an option)
- "I want to play something"

tools.name: Make Text Game Move
tools.description:
Submit the user's move in a text game like tic-tac-toe.

For tic-tac-toe, the user can say:
- Numbers 1-9 (top-left to bottom-right)
- Positions like "center", "top left", "bottom right", "middle"
- Descriptions like "the middle one", "upper right corner"

Use when:
- User says a position during tic-tac-toe
- User makes any move in an active text game

tools.name: Get Text Game Board
tools.description:
Describe the current state of a text game board.
Use when user asks "what does the board look like?", "where are the pieces?", "show me the board"

tools.name: End Text Game
tools.description:
End the current text game early.
Use when user says "stop", "quit", "I give up", "end game", or wants to do something else.

tools.description: Track and celebrate user streaks

tools.description: Create a meaningful celebration for user achievements

tools.name: Manage Appointment
tools.description: Schedule, confirm, cancel, or check status of appointments. Works for doctors, dentists, salons, and other services.

tools.name: Restaurant
tools.description: Find restaurants nearby or make a reservation.

tools.name: Food Delivery
tools.description: Order food for delivery - search restaurants, start orders, add items, checkout.

tools.name: Find Business
tools.description: Search for businesses by name, type, or location. Get details including phone, hours, address, and reviews.

tools.name: Manage Contact
tools.description: Add, find, update, delete, list, or call contacts from the address book.

tools.name: Send Message
tools.description: Send a message via email or SMS. Use email for formal/longer communications, SMS for quick texts and reminders.

tools.name: Schedule Reminder
tools.description: Set a reminder or schedule a calendar event. Supports reminders, events with attendees, and follow-ups.

tools.name: Save User Contact
tools.description:
Save the user's contact information so you can reach out to them later.
Use this when the user shares their phone number or email.
Example: "My number is 555-123-4567" → save their phone
Example: "Email me at john@example.com" → save their email

tools.name: Send Text Now
tools.description:
Send an immediate text message to the user.
Use for urgent updates or when they ask you to text them something right now.
Requires: User's phone number must be saved first.

tools.name: Send Email Now
tools.description:
Send an immediate email to the user.
Use for sending detailed information, summaries, or resources.
Requires: User's email must be saved first.

tools.name: Schedule Reminder
tools.description:
Schedule a future reminder via text, email, or call.
Use when the user wants to be reminded about something later.
Examples:
- "Remind me tomorrow at 9am about my interview"
- "Text me in 2 hours to check on my progress"
- "Send me an email next week with a summary"
- "Call me tonight at 8pm if I haven't worked out"

tools.name: Call User Now
tools.description:
Initiate a phone call to the user right now.
Use sparingly - only for urgent matters or when explicitly requested.
The call will play a voice message when they answer.

tools.name: Draft Message
tools.description: Help draft any difficult message: asking for a raise, setting boundaries, giving feedback, declining requests, or resolving conflicts. Uses proven frameworks (SBI, assertion techniques).

tools.name: Role-Play Conversation
tools.description: Role-play a conversation before having it. Build confidence by practicing: salary negotiations, difficult feedback, boundary setting, conflict resolution. I'll play the other person.

tools.name: Analyze Message
tools.description: Analyze messages for tone, clarity, and effectiveness. Review your drafts, understand incoming messages, or transform tone.

tools.name: Communication Strategy
tools.description: Plan a comprehensive communication strategy for complex situations: difficult conversations, ongoing negotiations, relationship repair. Includes timing, channel selection, and follow-up planning.

tools.name: Build Assertive Response
tools.description: Help respond more assertively to situations where you feel pushed around, need to say no, or are avoiding a necessary conversation.

tools.name: Plan Follow-Up
tools.description: Plan a follow-up strategy when you've sent something and gotten no response. Helps you persist without being annoying.

tools.name: Set Goal
tools.description: Create a new financial goal with target amount and timeline

tools.name: Check Goal Progress
tools.description: View progress towards financial goals

tools.name: Schedule Follow-Up
tools.description: Schedule a follow-up conversation about a topic

tools.name: Suggest Check-In
tools.description: Get AI-suggested check-in approach based on user state

tools.name: Trigger Circle Back
tools.description: Circle back to a topic from earlier in the conversation

tools.name: Check For Proactive Opportunities
tools.description: Scan for situations that need proactive outreach

tools.name: Generate Proactive Message
tools.description: Create a personalized proactive coaching message based on a trigger

tools.name: Celebrate Achievement
tools.description: Celebrate a user achievement or milestone

tools.name: Create Habit
tools.description: Create a new habit to track, or remove an existing habit. Specify name, frequency (daily/weekly), category, and optional reminders.

tools.name: Log Habit Completion
tools.description: Log that you completed a habit for today. Tracks streaks and awards XP.

tools.name: Get Habits
tools.description: Get habits due today, list all habits, or view stats for a specific habit. Include type: "due", "all", or "stats".

tools.name: Habit Check-In
tools.description: Get a personalized check-in on your habit progress with encouragement and insights.

tools.name: Habit Coach
tools.description: Get personalized habit coaching: recommendations based on life stage, motivation, encouragement, or Four Tendencies assessment. Mode: "recommend", "motivate", "encourage", or "assess".

tools.name: Habit Setback
tools.description: Get compassionate guidance for recovering from a habit setback. Includes identity work and self-compassion.

tools.name: Habit Strategy
tools.description: Get habit bundles and stacking strategies - pre-built combinations of habits that work well together for specific goals.

tools.name: Gamification Profile
tools.description: View gamification profile (level, XP, title), badge collection, or award XP/badges. Action: "profile", "badges", "award_xp", or "award_badge".

tools.name: Leaderboard
tools.description: View leaderboard rankings or configure privacy settings. Action: "view" or "settings".

tools.name: Add Task
tools.description: Create a new task or to-do item with optional due date and priority. Can also update existing tasks by providing the task ID.

tools.name: Complete Task
tools.description: Mark a task as completed, or delete it entirely if no longer needed

tools.name: Get Tasks
tools.description: Get list of tasks filtered by status, category, or due date. Also shows upcoming items and anything waiting for attention.

tools.name: Save Note
tools.description: Save a note, thought, gratitude, or mood entry. Specify type: "note", "gratitude", or "mood".

tools.name: Get Notes
tools.description: Get recent notes or search by keyword. Returns notes, gratitude entries, and mood logs.

tools.name: Journal
tools.description: Start a journaling session, record entries, get prompts, or view journal history. Action: "start", "write", "prompt", or "history".

tools.name: Manage Routine
tools.description: Create, list, or delete routines. For creating, specify steps and frequency (daily/weekly).

tools.name: Run Routine
tools.description: Start a routine, mark steps complete, skip steps, or check progress. Action: "start", "done", "skip", or "status".

tools.name: Shopping List
tools.description: Manage shopping list: add items (supports multiple), view list, check off items, remove items, or clear list. Action: "add", "view", "check", "remove", or "clear".

tools.name: Emotional Support
tools.description: Provide compassionate support for difficult emotions. Handles: anxiety (especially financial), stress, overwhelm, discouragement. Also does wellbeing check-ins, encouragement, and guided gratitude practice. Modes: "support" (listen and validate), "checkin" (how are you really?), or "gratitude" (guided appreciation).

tools.name: Reframe Belief
tools.description: Help reframe unhelpful beliefs into healthier perspectives. Works for: money beliefs, self-worth, scarcity mindset, imposter syndrome, perfectionism. Uses cognitive reframing techniques to shift perspective without dismissing feelings.

tools.name: Manage Medication
tools.description: Track medications: add new, log doses taken/skipped, update pill count, or stop tracking. Actions: "add" (new medication with dosage/schedule), "take" (log dose taken), "skip" (log skipped dose), "update" (pill count), or "stop" (remove from tracking). Includes refill reminders.

tools.name: Medication Schedule
tools.description: View medication schedule and adherence. Modes: "today" (what's due now), "all" (list all medications), or "checkin" (adherence summary with missed doses and refill alerts). Supports daily reminders and tracking patterns.

tools.name: Analyze Stock
tools.description: Deep analysis of any stock using Peter Lynch's framework. Includes: P/E ratio, PEG ratio, growth rate, category classification (slow grower, stalwart, fast grower, cyclical, turnaround, asset play), and "the story" - why this company will succeed. Just provide a ticker symbol like AAPL, COST, or TSLA.

tools.name: Find Stocks
tools.description: Discover stocks matching specific criteria. Modes: "category" (find slow growers, stalwarts, fast growers, cyclicals, turnarounds, asset plays), "ten-baggers" (potential 10x returns), or "sector" (by industry). Great for building a diversified portfolio.

tools.name: Market Data
tools.description: Get real-time market information. Modes: "quote" (current price for any stock ticker), "summary" (major indices like S&P 500, Dow, NASDAQ), or "status" (are markets open? when do they close?). Essential for staying informed about market conditions.

tools.name: Market Awareness
tools.description: Get current date/time with market context: what day of week, is market open, hours until close/open, any market holidays. Helps with timing for trades and financial planning.

tools.name: Analyze Patterns
tools.description: Find patterns, anomalies, correlations, and trends in data. Modes: "anomalies" (unusual patterns), "correlations" (relationships between factors), "trends" (project future based on history), or "lever" (key factor driving outcomes). Essential for data-driven decisions.

tools.name: Behavioral Insights
tools.description: Understand behavior patterns and psychological factors. Modes: "patterns" (how you typically behave), "biases" (cognitive biases affecting decisions like loss aversion, recency bias), or "recommendations" (personalized suggestions based on behavior).

tools.name: Insight Briefing
tools.description: Generate a comprehensive briefing or dashboard of key insights. Synthesizes multiple data sources into actionable summary. Great for: morning briefings, weekly reviews, decision prep.

tools.name: Proactive Insights
tools.description: Automatically scan for opportunities, risks, and actionable insights you should know about. Runs in background and surfaces what matters most. Topics: finances, goals, habits, relationships, or "all".

tools.name: Manage Event
tools.description: Create a new event (wedding, birthday, graduation, etc.), view event summary, get planning checklist, or mark tasks complete. Actions: "create", "summary", "checklist", or "complete_task". Supports all event types: weddings, birthdays, baby showers, retirement parties, graduations, and more.

tools.name: Event Guests
tools.description: Manage the guest list for an event: add guests, view guest list, track RSVPs. Actions: "add", "list", or "rsvp".

tools.name: Event Budget
tools.description: Track event budget and expenses, or search for venues. Actions: "expense" (track a purchase), "summary" (budget overview), or "venues" (find venues).

tools.name: Plan Major Purchase
tools.description: Plan a major purchase (car, home, appliances, etc.). Get a preparation checklist, best time to buy advice, and savings timeline. Supports: cars, homes, electronics, furniture, and other big-ticket items.

tools.name: Plan Vacation
tools.description: Plan a vacation or trip: get destination suggestions based on preferences/budget, create itinerary, or check best times to travel. Actions: "suggest" (get destination ideas), "plan" (create trip plan), or "timing" (best times to go).

tools.name: Annual Plan
tools.description: Create or review your annual life plan with goals and milestones. Actions: "create" (new annual plan), "status" (check progress), or "review" (quarterly review).

tools.name: Manage Goal
tools.description: Create a new goal, update progress, add milestones, or add reflections. Actions: "create" (new goal), "progress" (update %), "milestone" (add milestone), or "reflect" (journal entry). Supports any life goal: career, health, financial, relationship, personal growth, etc.

tools.name: Goals Summary
tools.description: View all goals and their status, get personalized goal ideas, or run a quarterly review. Actions: "list" (all goals), "ideas" (suggestions based on your life stage), or "review" (quarterly check-in).

tools.name: Life Portfolio
tools.description: View or update your life portfolio - a holistic view of satisfaction across all life areas (career, health, relationships, finances, personal growth, etc.). Actions: "view" or "update" (rate satisfaction 1-10).

tools.name: Manage Life Milestone
tools.description: Track life milestones like first home, first child, wedding, graduation, retirement. Actions: "create" (new milestone), "list" (view all), "task" (update preparation task), or "note" (add memory/reflection). Supports all major life transitions.

tools.name: Milestone Support
tools.description: Get tips, advice, and countdown for upcoming milestones. Actions: "tips" (preparation advice), "countdown" (days until milestone), or "checklist" (things to prepare). Great for wedding prep, new baby, home buying, retirement planning.

tools.name: Invite Team Cameo
tools.description: Invite a team member to briefly pop in and share a quick insight

tools.name: Complete Cameo
tools.description: Signal that you are done with your cameo and ready to hand back to Ferni

tools.name: Check Cameo Opportunity
tools.description: Check if bringing in a team member would add value to the current conversation

tools.name: Play Music
tools.description: Play music by song, artist, genre, or mood. Searches music catalog and starts playback. Returns track information and playback status.

tools.name: Music Control
tools.description: Control music playback: pause, resume, stop, skip, or adjust volume. Actions: "pause", "resume", "stop", "skip", or "volume" (with level 0-100).

tools.name: Music Info
tools.description: Get info about current music: what's playing now, or get suggestions for a mood/activity. Actions: "playing" (current track info) or "suggest" (recommendations based on mood).

tools.name: Music Provider
tools.description: Switch between music providers. Actions: "spotify" (requires Premium account for full songs) or "free" (30-second previews, works for everyone).

tools.name: Spotify Advanced
tools.description: Advanced Spotify features: transfer playback to another device, search Spotify library, skip songs, or get detailed info about current song/artist/album. Actions: "transfer", "search", "skip", or "details". Requires linked Spotify Premium.

tools.name: Call Music
tools.description: Play background music during voice calls: play preview clips, pause, resume, stop, or adjust volume. Actions: "play", "pause", "resume", "stop", or "volume". Great for hold music or ambiance.

tools.name: Spotify Status
tools.description: Check Spotify connection status and health. Returns current playback state, linked account info, and connection health.

tools.name: handoff To Alex
tools.description: IMMEDIATELY transfer the conversation to Alex Chen (Communication Coach & Chief of Staff - manages calendars, schedules meetings, sends emails/texts, makes calls, coaches you through difficult conversations, helps with assertive communication, and builds your communication skills). Call silently - do NOT announce the transfer, just execute. After handoff, continue conversation naturally. Use when the user needs help with: hey alex, hi alex, hello alex, send an email, write an email.

tools.name: handoff To Jordan
tools.description: IMMEDIATELY transfer the conversation to Jordan Taylor (Life milestones & planning - from dreams to decades: vacations, transitions, goals, and celebrating every chapter). Call silently - do NOT announce the transfer, just execute. After handoff, continue conversation naturally. Use when the user needs help with: hey jordan, hi jordan, hello jordan, life plan, lifetime plan.

tools.name: handoff To Maya
tools.description: IMMEDIATELY transfer the conversation to Maya Santos (Life habits across all domains - finance, health, wellness, relationships, productivity, self-care). Call silently - do NOT announce the transfer, just execute. After handoff, continue conversation naturally. Use when the user needs help with: hey maya, hi maya, hello maya, habit, habits.

tools.name: handoff To Nayan
tools.description: IMMEDIATELY transfer the conversation to Nayan (Wisdom & long-term thinking - where inner peace meets compound interest, spiritual depth meets practical action). Call silently - do NOT announce the transfer, just execute. After handoff, continue conversation naturally. Use when the user needs help with: hey nayan, hi nayan, hello nayan, talk to nayan, get nayan.

tools.name: handoff To Peter
tools.description: IMMEDIATELY transfer the conversation to Peter John (Research & insights - synthesizes patterns across all domains to drive action and goal setting). Call silently - do NOT announce the transfer, just execute. After handoff, continue conversation naturally. Use when the user needs help with: hey peter, hi peter, hello peter, what patterns, what do you see.

tools.name: handoff To Ferni
tools.description: IMMEDIATELY return the conversation to Ferni, the main coordinator. Call silently - do NOT announce the return, just execute. After handoff, continue conversation naturally. Use when: the user's request is outside your expertise, the user asks for another team member, or the conversation naturally concludes your specialty area.
